content:
Key Findings

  • West Lancashire Borough Council's Housing Revenue Account spent £109.4M over 10 years
  • This department accounted for 32.9% of the council's total spending
  • The next highest spending department was Coronavirus business grants at £21.0M

Housing Revenue Account: A Major Spending Department

West Lancashire Borough Council's Housing Revenue Account is its largest spending department, accounting for £109.4M over the past decade. This represents 32.9% of the council's total expenditure of £332.9M. The Housing Revenue Account is a ring-fenced budget used to manage and maintain the council's housing stock, including repairs, improvements, and day-to-day management of council-owned properties.

The significant share of spending highlights the council's focus on housing services. With £109.4M allocated to this department, it surpasses other major departments such as Environmental Services and Corporate and Customer Services. This emphasis reflects the council's commitment to maintaining and improving its housing stock to meet the needs of its residents.

Comparing Departmental Spending

The Housing Revenue Account's spending of £109.4M is more than five times that of the next highest spending department, Coronavirus business grants, which accounted for £21.0M. This stark contrast underscores the priority given to housing services within the council's budget. Other significant departments include Other Service Items at £20.3M and Environmental Services at £18.0M.

The distribution of spending across departments provides insight into the council's priorities. While the Housing Revenue Account dominates, other departments such as Housing Public Sector and Corporate Services also receive substantial funding. This balanced approach ensures that various services are adequately supported, although the focus on housing is evident.

Rank Department Amount % of Total
1 Housing Revenue Account £109.4M 32.9%
2 Coronavirus business grants £21.0M 6.3%
3 Other Service Items £20.3M 6.1%
4 Environmental Services £18.0M 5.4%
5 N/A £17.1M 5.1%

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to West Lancashire Borough Council about the specific projects funded by the Housing Revenue Account
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about housing initiatives and other departmental activities
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see which suppliers are involved in housing projects
  • Write to your local councillor about the allocation of funds to the Housing Revenue Account and other departments
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse trends over the past decade
This article is based on West Lancashire Borough Council's published spending data covering 2016/17 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
